questions.q: What is meant by budget deficit?
questions.answer: A budget deficit occurs when the government expenditure exceeds its revenue.

questions.q: What is the main objective of the budget?
questions.answer: The main objective of the budget is to bring about an equilibrium between the government's income and expenditure.

questions.q: State any two types of budget.
questions.answer: Two types of budget are: Revenu e Budget and Expenditure Budget.
A budget circular is a document that outlines the budgetary procedure.

questions.q: What is meant by budgetary control?
questions.answer: Budgetary control is the process of controlling and evaluating the actual performance of a department in relation to its budget.

questions.q: What is the difference between government budget and private sector budget?
questions.answer: The government budget is a comprehensive plan of taxation and public expenditure, while the private sector budget is a financial plan for an individual or organization.
key_points: Government budget vs. private sector budget

questions.q: Explain the concept of budget deficit financing.
questions.answer: Budget deficit financing refers to the process of financing a budget deficit by borrowing, printing money, or reducing savings. It can be achieved through internal sources (taxation) or external sources (borrowing).

questions.q: Distinguish between a budget surplus and a budget deficit.
questions.answer: A budget surplus occurs when the government's revenue exceeds its expenditure, while a budget deficit occurs when the government's expenditure exceeds its revenue.

questions.q: Describe the main functions of a budget.
questions.answer: The main functions of a budget are: (i) Allocation of resources, (ii) Determination of priorities, (iii) Control of expenditure, (iv) Achievement of macro-economic goals, and (v) Maintenance of fiscal discipline.

questions.q: Explain the concept of budgetary control.
questions.answer: Budgetary control refers to the process of controlling and evaluating the actual performance of a department in relation to its budget. It involves comparing the actual performance with the budgeted performance and taking corrective action to achieve the budgeted goals.

questions.q: What are the advantages of budgetary control?
questions.answer: The advantages of budgetary control are: (i) Helps in achieving efficiency and economy in administration, (ii) Facilitates effective planning and decision-making, (iii) Promotes transparency and accountability, and (iv) Enhances the credibility of the government.

questions.q: Explain the concept of fiscal policy.
questions.answer: Fiscal policy refers to the use of government revenue and expenditure as a tool to achieve macro-economic goals such as economic growth, employment, and price stability. It involves the use of taxation and public expenditure to influence aggregate demand and supply.

questions.q: Discuss the importance of budget in the economy.
questions.answer: The budget is important in the economy because it: (i) Helps in achieving macro-economic goals, (ii) Influences the level of economic activity, (iii) Affects the distribution of income, and (iv) Provides a framework for decision-making.

questions.q: Explain the concept of budgetary reform.
questions.answer: Budgetary reform refers to the process of changing the budgetary system to make it more effective and efficient. It involves reforms such as decentralization, computerization, and performance budgeting.

questions.q: What is the difference between a revenue budget and an expenditure budget?
questions.answer: A revenue budget shows the estimated revenue of the government, while an expenditure budget shows the estimated expenditure of the government.

questions.q: Explain the concept of budgetary control in a business organization.
questions.answer: Budgetary control in a business organization refers to the process of controlling and evaluating the actual performance of a department in relation to its budget. It involves comparing the actual performance with the budgeted performance and taking corrective action to achieve the budgeted goals.
